intent: A project management platform that utilizes predictive AI to analyze task velocity, identify bottleneck risks before they occur, and automatically rebalance sprint workloads based on team historical capacity and real-time dependency tracking.

entities.name: VelocityMetricEngine
entities.description: A stream-processing component that calculates rolling averages and standard deviations of task completion rates based on historical repository metadata.

entities.name: DependencyGraphNode
entities.description: An entity representing a unit of work that contains topological linkage data, tracking inter-task dependencies across microservices or project boards.

entities.name: CapacityProfile
entities.description: A high-dimensional representation of a developer's historical output, factoring in time-zone availability, leave status, and specialized skill proficiency tags.

entities.name: BottleneckPredictor
entities.description: A machine learning inference model that monitors real-time changes in task duration versus baseline expectations to flag high-risk stalled items.

entities.name: SprintOrchestrator
entities.description: The logic layer that executes the workload rebalancing algorithm, outputting recommended task redistribution proposals for the team.

entities.name: SyncAdapter
entities.description: An abstraction layer that normalizes event schemas from disparate external systems like Jira and GitHub into a unified internal schema for analysis.

entities.name: WorkloadSnapshot
entities.description: A point-in-time state representation of all active sprints, used for comparing predictive model accuracy against realized sprint outcomes.

architecture.description: The system architecture utilizes a reactive event-driven model where the SyncAdapter consumes webhooks and API events from external issue trackers, normalizing them into immutable event streams. These streams are ingested by the VelocityMetricEngine to update team performance benchmarks. Concurrently, the DependencyGraphNode maintains a live map of task constraints. The BottleneckPredictor continuously evaluates the WorkloadSnapshot against the CapacityProfile; when a threshold of delay is detected, it triggers the SprintOrchestrator. The orchestrator rebalances assignments by applying a constraint-satisfaction algorithm that optimizes for throughput while respecting individual CapacityProfile limits, finally pushing the suggested adjustments back to the external repositories via the SyncAdapter.

endpoints.summary: Ingest normalized event data from external systems
validation_rules: raw_event_data must be a valid JSON object
developer_id must be a valid string identifier

endpoints.summary: Update developer skill tags or leave status
validation_rules: timezone_offset must be a valid real number

endpoints.summary: Fetch topological linkage data for a specific task
Retrieve historical performance benchmarks for a team
validation_rules: team_id must be a valid identifier

endpoints.summary: Create a new point-in-time state representation of sprints
validation_rules: state_data must be provided as a non-empty JSON object

endpoints.summary: Fetch list of high-risk stalled items for a snapshot
validation_rules: snapshot_id must be a valid integer
proposal_id must be a valid integer

endpoints.summary: Update status of an orchestration proposal
validation_rules: status must be one of: pending, approved, rejected

components.content: Overview of Velocity Metrics and Sprint Snapshots
Update Developer Skills and Leave Status
List of Proposed Workload Rebalancing Adjustments
Stalled Items Identified by Bottleneck Predictor

rules.description: Validate that all incoming external events in sync_adapters contain mandatory fields before processing.
rules.logic: IF sync_adapters.raw_event_data is missing entity_id, timestamp, or event_type, reject the ingestion.

rules.description: Prevent circular task dependencies in dependency_graph_nodes.
rules.logic: Perform a topological sort on dependency_graph_nodes.dependencies whenever a new edge is added; if a cycle is detected, block the update.

rules.description: Enforce capacity constraints for developers within the sprint_orchestrators logic.
rules.logic: If the sum of task complexity scores in sprint_orchestrators.redistribution_plan exceeds the developer's capacity_profiles limits, mark proposal as rejected.

rules.description: Verify bottleneck probability thresholds using historical data from velocity_metric_engines.
rules.logic: A task in bottleneck_predictors is flagged as stalled only if the duration variance exceeds three standard deviations defined in velocity_metric_engines.
Any update attempt to an existing record in workload_snapshots is prohibited; only new insertions are allowed for state changes.

rules.description: Confirm external synchronization success before finalizing sprint_orchestrators status.
rules.logic: Only update sprint_orchestrators.status to approved after the sync_adapters confirms successful external transmission.
